name: Butter-Poached Blue Prawns with Saffron-Shellfish Emulsion
description: A molecular gastronomy course featuring precision-temperature prawns and a centrifuged saffron-shellfish foam.
keywords: prawns, shrimp, molecular gastronomy, sous vide

steps.instruction: Prepare the shellfish stock using prawn heads and shells.
Pour the strained stock into the canisters of a culinary centrifuge; spin until the liquid clarifies into a pure essence.
Place butter, saffron, and lemongrass into a vacuum bag; use a chamber vacuum sealer to pull a full vacuum for precision infusion.
Clean prawns and place in a vacuum bag with the saffron-infused butter; seal and submerge in the water bath for 12�15 minutes.
Reduce the centrifuged shellfish essence with white wine, using a digital refractometer to verify the ideal density/sugar concentration.
Add lecithin or heavy cream to the reduction; pour into a siphon and charge with gas cartridges.
Remove prawns from the bath, pat dry, and give a brief singe with a culinary torch for aroma.
Plating: Place prawns in a shallow bowl, dispense saffron foam from the siphon over the top, and garnish with chervil.
